commits: d7bbea6: test(0.5.2-COMPAT): design memo for cross-cutting capability-compat suite
da70a3c: test(0.5.2-COMPAT): lock cross-cutting capability/naming contract vs real config
f8eb4cb: chore(0.5.2-COMPAT): closure artifact (output.json) — 43/43 green, tests-only
9b41c2a: test(0.5.2-COMPAT): harden assertions per codex review (stronger regression-lock)

tdd_evidence.note: Regression-lock pack against already-shipped code: the suite is GREEN on first write, so there is no separate RED tests-only commit. Each load-bearing assertion (including the post-review HARDENED ones) was proven able to go RED by inverting the expected value / simulating a regression, then restoring (no product change). Inversion evidence: (1) collision-safety resolve('gemini-3.1-pro-preview') real=None, inverted assert =="x" FAILS; (2) old<->new parity infer_provider('vertex/gemini-3.5-flash') real='vertex_ai', inverted assert =='gemini' FAILS; (3) capability<->wiring forcing every entry to expect batch yields 63/73 mismatches (FAILS). HARDENED-area RED: (4) Area-4 full-list — over-claiming an extra endpoint on a chat-only entry passes the OLD 'batch in endpoints' check but FAILS the new full-list equality; (5) Area-5 independent oracle — simulating capability_record dropping 'deprecated' makes record != _EXPECTED_CAP literal so the four-way equality FAILS, even though both product surfaces still mirror the broken record (the old circular check would have passed).

blockers_encountered.description: First run: 4 TestAliasParity cases failed with infer_provider(...) == None for anthropic/, vertex/, vllm/, and aistudio-prefixed aliases. Root cause: the autouse conftest fixture tests/conftest.py::_reset_router_catalog calls set_router_config(None) before every test function, emptying router._alias_provider_map AFTER my originally module-scoped 'wired' fixture had populated it. infer_provider then fell back to the prefix-only heuristic (_PROVIDER_PREFIXES), which covers mistral but not anthropic/vertex_ai/openai-via-vllm. Test-harness ordering interaction, NOT a product bug (alias_table.resolve assertions passed because the autouse reset only touches the router catalog, not the alias table).
blockers_encountered.resolution: Made the 'wired' fixture function-scoped so it re-applies set_router_config(cfg) AFTER the autouse reset runs for each test. Suite went green; no product/config change.

blockers_encountered.description: Codex review of the suite returned CONCERN with 5 findings, all test-side (a regression-lock that cannot fail is worthless) — no product bug. (1) Area 5 was circular: capability_record was the oracle for BOTH product surfaces, which are themselves built from capability_record, so a regression INSIDE capability_record would still pass. (2) Injected-model_info test only checked 'expected keys present', not exact — stale/extra keys would pass. (3) Area 4 only checked 'batch in endpoints_for' — extra/garbage endpoints would pass. (4) Area 1 only checked infer_provider parity + legacy non-None resolve, not that both aliases RESOLVE through alias_table to the same underlying. (5) 'wired' fixture never restored global singleton state on teardown, risking session leak.
blockers_encountered.resolution: All 5 fixed in commit 9b41c2a (test-only, no product/config change, suite stays green at 54 passed): (1)+(2) added a hand-written _EXPECTED_CAP oracle (4 representative entries) and a four-way equality test (injected == seam-map == capability_record == literal); the per-entry injected-model_info check is now EXACT whole-dict equality. (3) Area 4 now asserts the FULL endpoints list per entry (==['chat'(,'batch')]). (4) Area 1 now asserts both old+new aliases resolve through alias_table to the SAME underlying litellm deployment. (5) 'wired' is a yield fixture that restores router map (set_router_config(None)) + resets the alias_table singleton on teardown. Re-confirmed inversion-RED for the hardened area-4 and area-5 assertions (see tdd_evidence). Neighbor isolation run (test_model_alias, test_capability, test_models_seam, test_fast_router) green => no leak.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: wired fixture: module->function scope, then converted to a yield fixture with teardown restoring both global singletons (router map + alias_table). Test-internal only; keeps catalog-first infer_provider lookups order-independent vs the autouse router-reset conftest fixture and prevents session leak.
Added _EXPECTED_CAP independent oracle + four-way agreement test; tightened Area 4 (full endpoints list), Area 5 (exact model_info dict), and Area 1 (resolve-to-same-underlying) per codex review.
agent_verify_result: pass — uv run --extra test python -m pytest tests/test_0_5_2_capability_compat.py -q => 54 passed. Neighbor isolation run (test_0_5_2_capability_compat, test_model_alias, test_capability, test_models_seam, test_fast_router) => 309 passed, no leak. ruff check + ruff format --check clean.
next_step_for_orchestrator: Pack 0.5.2-COMPAT-tests on branch feat/0.5.2-COMPAT-tests at 9b41c2a; review, verify, merge. Tests-only, zero product/config changes. Codex CONCERN (5 hardening findings) addressed in 9b41c2a.
